title: lotodes candicans
intro: Lotodes candicans, also known as the Indian lotus or water lily, is a type of aquatic plant that grows in shallow waters and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Lotodes candicans include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the roots and leaves in hot water or oil, and can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
